verdict.note: 7/7: claude-shaped payloads, agent_id prefix order, block semantics, launch branching, real deliverViaRendezvous parse-confirmed
init grows .opencode plugin+skills+exclude; narrowing harnesses prunes and sweeps .opencode itself

Reproduced: second send to an opencode worker under normal board-probe pressure exits 1 ('kicked by concurrent connects 3× — prompt NOT delivered') yet the prompt LANDED — opencode's db shows 2 duplicate user injections from the one send (deliver's own retries), pane shows 3 SECOND-OK answer turns. Root cause: the generated plugin daemon awaits injectPrompt INSIDE the line-parse loop, so repaint-done is never written before a probe connect kicks the connection — the parse-confirmed contract (harness.ts replyViaSocket) is broken on the daemon side.

note: mis-anchored: scenario declaration was uncommitted at filing; re-filing after the declaration commit

verdict.note: Reproduced: second send to an opencode worker under normal board-probe pressure exits 1 ('kicked by concurrent connects 3× — prompt NOT delivered') yet the prompt LANDED — opencode's db shows 2 duplicate user injections from the one send (deliver's own retries), pane shows 3 SECOND-OK answer turns. Root cause: the generated plugin daemon awaits injectPrompt INSIDE the line-parse loop, so repaint-done is never written before a probe connect kicks the connection — the parse-confirmed contract (harness.ts replyViaSocket) is broken on the daemon side.
pi-dispatched worker cross-validated generated opencode plugin footprint, event/rendezvous bridge, launch tail branching, and 8/8 mechanical tests

Fixed: same scenario, same probe pressure (0.3s board snapshots), fresh opencode worker with the regenerated plugin — send exits 0 ('sent'), opencode's message store shows exactly ONE injected user message, pane shows one prompt + one SECOND-OK answer. Daemon now parse-confirms synchronously (repaint-done at parse time, injection behind the confirm); unit regression drives the real deliver under a kicking connect storm with a turn-length prompt call — fails on the old generator, passes now (9/9, tsc clean). A/B pair with the 06:24Z fail reading.
Re-measured after the daemon parse-confirm fix: full suite 9/9 (now includes the probe-pressure regression — kicking connects + turn-length prompt call → parse-confirmed ok, ONE injection).

Re-measured after the generator change: real spex init --harness claude,codex,opencode in a scratch repo materializes .opencode/plugins/spexcode.ts (carrying the FIXED sync parse-confirm daemon, dispatch.sh baked), skills dir, AGENTS.md managed block, all exclude-hidden; narrowing harnesses to claude,codex + spex materialize prunes every .opencode artifact and sweeps the emptied home.
